title_en: Texan Neo-Noir Returns as Image Comics Reveals That Texas Blood: Hell Comes to Allison Ranch
summary_en: Image Comics' critical darling That Texas Blood is back with a sequel series called That Texas Blood: Hell Comes to Allison Ranch. Check out our exclusive preview of the first chapter.
body_en:
Chris Condon and Jacob Phillips' That Texas Blood has easily been one of the biggest hits from Image Comics in recent years, and it's not hard to see why. The original series is basically a fusion of Cormac McCarthy and John Carpenter, delivering a neo-noir Western unlike any other. The series has even proven popular enough that it's now being adapted as a live-action FX show . With that in mind, it's great to learn that Condon and Phillips are collaborating on a sequel.
IGN can exclusively reveal That Texas Blood: Hell Comes to Allison Ranch. This new series again follows the existentially troubled Sheriff Joe Bob Coates as he embarks on a violent new case at the titular ranch. Check out the slideshow gallery below for a first look at the new series:
Here's Image's official description of That Texas Blood: Hell Comes to Allison Ranch:
"We're back! Jacob and I were set to release That Texas Blood 's latest volume but decided to hold back the release and put together a package that was bigger and better than That Texas Blood has ever been before," Condon tells IGN. "This new issue will kick off a new miniseries set in 2003 and will be jam-packed with bonus materials, including interviews with Spencer Ackerman (Forever Wars, Reign of Terror: How the 9/11 Era Destabilized America and Produced Trump), Jim Mickle and E.L. Katz who will be adapting the book for television, a new era-specific cartoon by Matt Bors, and more. We are so excited to be returning to That Texas Blood with our new teammates Hassan Otsmane-Elhaou and Michael Tivey, and feel strongly that this may just be the best issue we've produced yet. You won't want to miss a beat—trust me."
“After the incredibly exciting announcement of the That Texas Blood TV show we are doubly excited for the comic to be returning with a brand new miniseries," says Phillips. "This oversized issue full of extra goodies is the perfect jumping-on point for new readers as well as warm welcome back to Ambrose County for those that have been with us from the start. Join us and Sheriff Joe Bob Coates and come along for the ride!"
That Texas Blood: Hell Comes to Allison Ranch #1 will be released on September 23. You can preorder a copy at your local comic shop.
For more, check out Marvel's newest Avengers roster and see an exclusive preview of Tom Morello's latest comic book .
Jesse is a mild-mannered staff writer for IGN. Allow him to lend a machete to your intellectual thicket by following @jschedeen on BlueSky .
